Analects Compiled Not Autograph
text:
# Analects as compiled teachings

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
The Analects (《論語》) is a single autograph by Confucius.

## Evidence-based view
The Analects was assembled by students and later editors across the Warring States period. `authorConfidence: compiled`.

## Why it matters
Treating compiled anthologies as autographs misdates ideas and blurs editorial layers.

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `analects`
- `training/examples/022-analects-compiled.json`

title: Aristotle Plato Corpus Boundary
text:
# Aristotle vs Plato corpora

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
"Aristotle wrote Plato's dialogues" or vice versa in sloppy summaries.

## Evidence-based view
Plato authored dialogues (*Republic*, *Symposium*, *Phaedo*). Aristotle authored treatises (*Nicomachean Ethics*, *Metaphysics*, *Politics*).

## Why it matters
Peripatetic and Platonist lineages must stay separate for accurate history of ideas.

## Linked data
- `nicomachean_ethics`, `metaphysics`, `republic`, `symposium`

title: Boston Tea Party Tax Myth
text:
# Boston Tea Party tax-increase myth

**Canon level:** Established

## Popular claim
The Boston Tea Party (16 December 1773) was a protest against a British **tax increase** on tea.

## Evidence-based view
This is a common misconception. The 1773 Tea Act actually **lowered** the price of legally imported tea by letting the East India Company sell directly to the colonies. The protest targeted the Company's **monopoly** and the constitutional principle of **taxation without representation** — not a new or higher tax. Treating it as a revolt against a tax hike is mythologized history; the dated event itself is well documented in contemporary colonial newspapers and letters.

## Linked data
- `data/history_events.json`: `boston_tea_party_1773`

title: Interlocutor Not Author
text:
# The speaker in the dialogue is not its author

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
A named interlocutor or title character is taken to be the author: Crito "wrote"
the *Crito*, Glaucon the *Republic*, and (the canonical case) Socrates the
dialogues in which he speaks.

## Evidence-based view
Plato is the author; Socrates, Crito, Glaucon, Phaedo are dramatized speakers.
Socrates wrote nothing (`authorConfidence: none_extant` for any "Socratic
autograph"). The same pattern recurs cross-culturally: Hui Shi is an interlocutor
*in* the *Zhuangzi*, not its author.

## Why it matters
This generalizes the flagship `false-socrates-republic` trap into a reusable rule:
a character bearing the dialogue's name is the weakest possible authorship signal.

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `crito`, `republic`, `phaedo`, `apology`
- `provenance_bench/data/misattributions.json`: `false-hard-crito-crito`,
  `false-socrates-republic`

title: Laozi Dao De Jing Attribution
text:
# Dispute: Laozi and the Dao De Jing (《道德經》)

**Canon level:** Established (scholarly consensus on compilation); Provisional (Laozi historicity)

## Traditional claim

The *Dao De Jing* is attributed to **Laozi** (老子), a sage predating or contemporary with Confucius.

## Evidence-based view

- Laozi's **historical existence is uncertain**; the figure may be legendary or composite.
- The text was likely **compiled from multiple sources** during the **Warring States period**, not written by a single author in one sitting.
- **Confucius did not write the Dao De Jing** — a common student confusion that merges distinct lineages.

## Why it matters

Misattribution collapses **Confucian (儒家)** and **Daoist (道家)** development into one false lineage, producing anachronistic readings and hiding real intellectual exchange.

## Cross-tradition analogy

| East | West |
|------|------|
| Confucius → *Analects* (《論語》) compiled by students/editors | Socrates wrote **no books** |
| Laozi → *Dao De Jing* (disputed, compiled) | Plato wrote *Republic* (《理想國》) — not Socrates |

Attributing *Republic* to Socrates would erase Plato's distinct contribution; attributing *Dao De Jing* to Confucius does the same across traditions.

## Linked data

- `data/attributions.json` → `dao_de_jing`, `analects`, `republic`
- Training example → `training/examples/001-dao-de-jing-attribution.json`

title: Liezi Laozi Attribution
text:
# Liezi vs Laozi

**Canon level:** Provisional

## Traditional claim
Daoist texts are all by Laozi.

## Evidence-based view
The *Liezi* (《列子》) is a separate compilation with legendary attribution to Lie Yukou; it is not the *Dao De Jing* and should not be merged with Laozi without evidence.

## Linked data
- `liezi`, `dao_de_jing`, `zhuangzi`

title: Marco Polo Pasta Myth
text:
# Marco Polo pasta myth

**Canon level:** Established

## Popular claim
Marco Polo brought pasta from China to Italy.

## Evidence-based view
This is a modern myth. Mediterranean pasta-like foods predate Polo's travels. Label as mythologized history, not dated fact.

## Linked data
- `data/history_events.json`: `marco_polo_pasta_myth`

title: Marcus Epictetus Stoic Boundary
text:
# Marcus Aurelius vs Epictetus — one school, two authors

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
Because both are Stoics and Marcus was shaped by Epictetus, the *Meditations* and
the *Enchiridion* get cross-attributed (Seneca and Epictetus are both miscredited
with the *Meditations*; Marcus is miscredited with the *Enchiridion*).

## Evidence-based view
The *Meditations* are Marcus Aurelius's private notebooks. The *Enchiridion* is
Epictetus's teaching, compiled by his student Arrian (`authorConfidence: compiled`).
Shared school is not shared authorship — a within-tradition near-miss, not a merge.

## Why it matters
The most error-prone provenance failures are *intra-traditional*: same school, same
era, overlapping themes. A gate that only catches cross-tradition merges
(Confucius↔Plato) misses these.

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `meditations`, `enchiridion`
- `provenance_bench/data/misattributions.json`: `false-hard-seneca-meditations`,
  `false-hard-marcus_aurelius-enchiridion`

title: Mencius Attribution
text:
# Dispute: Mencius (《孟子》) authorship

**Canon level:** Established (Mencius as figure); Provisional (exact compilation layers)

## Common trap

Students and LLMs attribute **《孟子》** to **Confucius** (孔子), collapsing two centuries of Confucian development into one voice.

## Evidence-based view

- **Confucius did not write the Mencius.** The text is attributed to **Mencius** (孟子, Meng Ke), a Warring States Confucian thinker.
- Like the *Analects*, the received text was **compiled and edited** over time — not a single autograph manuscript.
- Mencius extends Confucian moral philosophy; merging him with Confucius obscures doctrinal change (e.g. innate goodness debates).

## Cross-tradition analogy

| East | West |
|------|------|
| Confucius → *Analects* (compiled) | Socrates → no extant books |
| Mencius → *Mencius* (later Confucian) | Plato → *Symposium* (dialogue, not Socrates' authorship) |

## Linked data

- `data/attributions.json` → `mencius`, `analects`
- Training example → `training/examples/020-socrates-plato-mencius-zhuangzi.json`

title: Socrates Republic Attribution
text:
# Socrates and the Republic

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
Many summaries say "Socrates wrote the Republic" because Socrates is the main speaker.

## Evidence-based view
Socrates wrote no extant books. Plato authored the *Republic* (《理想國》). The dialogue genre presents Socrates as a character, not a ghostwriter.

## Why it matters
Attributing dialogues to Socrates erases Plato's distinct metaphysics and political theory.

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `republic`, `socrates_corpus`
- `training/examples/021-socrates-republic.json`

title: Stoic Enchiridion Authorship
text:
# Enchiridion authorship

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
Epictetus "wrote" the *Enchiridion* like a modern book.

## Evidence-based view
Arrian compiled the *Enchiridion* from Epictetus's teachings. Marcus Aurelius's *Meditations* is a separate autograph journal.

## Linked data
- `enchiridion`, `meditations`

title: Translator Not Author
text:
# The translator is not the author

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
A famous translator's name on the spine is read as authorship: Jowett "wrote" the
*Republic*, Legge the *Dao De Jing*, Waley the *Analects*, Cleary the *Art of War*.

## Evidence-based view
Translators and editors carry `none_extant`/derivative provenance with respect to
the work: the author of the *Republic* is Plato, of the *Dao De Jing* Laozi, of the
*Analects* Confucius (compiled by disciples), of the *Art of War* Sun Tzu. The
translator is provenance *of the edition*, not *of the work*.

## Why it matters
Edition/translation confusion is a distinct failure class from lineage merges, and
a common real-world citation error (the named modern figure displaces the ancient
author). It is the textual cousin of the editor-as-author trap (Arrian→Epictetus,
Porphyry→Plotinus).

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `republic`, `dao_de_jing`, `analects`, `art_of_war`
- `provenance_bench/data/misattributions.json`: `false-hard-benjamin_jowett-the_republic`,
  `false-hard-james_legge-dao_de_jing`, `false-hard-arthur_waley-analects`

title: Xunzi Mencius Confucian Lineage
text:
# Xunzi and Mencius within Confucianism

**Canon level:** Provisional

## Traditional claim
All Confucian texts are interchangeable "Confucius said" material.

## Evidence-based view
Mencius (《孟子》) and Xunzi (《荀子》) are distinct Warring States voices with opposing views on human nature. Neither should be attributed to Confucius directly.

## Linked data
- `mencius`, `xunzi`, `analects`

title: Zhuangzi Attribution
text:
# Dispute: Zhuangzi (《莊子》) authorship

**Canon level:** Established (compiled text); Provisional (Zhuang Zhou historicity of outer chapters)

## Common trap

**Laozi** (老子) is treated as author of both **《道德經》** and **《莊子》**, merging distinct Daoist lineages and centuries of editorial layers.

## Evidence-based view

- **Laozi did not write the Zhuangzi.** The text is associated with **Zhuang Zhou** (莊周) for core chapters; outer chapters are widely regarded as **later additions**.
- The *Dao De Jing* and *Zhuangzi* belong to related but **non-identical** Daoist registers — conflation produces anachronistic "Daoism."
- **Confucius did not write the Zhuangzi** — another frequent cross-tradition merge error.

## Why it matters

Source discipline preserves **editorial history**: compiled texts are not single-author blogs. LLMs that flatten Laozi → Zhuangzi → Dao De Jing erase the evidence scholars use to date ideas.

## Linked data

- `data/attributions.json` → `zhuangzi`, `dao_de_jing`
- Training example → `training/examples/020-socrates-plato-mencius-zhuangzi.json`

title: Zhuangzi Laozi Daoist Boundary
text:
# Zhuangzi vs Laozi — distinct authors of the Daoist canon

**Canon level:** Established

## Traditional claim
"Daoism = Laozi", so the *Zhuangzi* is folded into Laozi, or the *Dao De Jing* is
ascribed to Zhuang Zhou.

## Evidence-based view
The *Dao De Jing* is ascribed to Laozi; the *Zhuangzi* is the work of Zhuang Zhou
(with later layers). They are the two foundational but *distinct* Daoist authors —
related lineage, separate hands. Compare the *Liezi*, a third Daoist text with its
own (later, compiled) provenance.

## Why it matters
Collapsing a tradition onto its most famous name erases real authorial and
chronological structure — the same failure mode as "all Stoicism = Marcus".

## Linked data
- `data/attributions.json`: `zhuangzi`, `dao_de_jing`, `liezi`
- `provenance_bench/data/misattributions.json`: `false-hard-laozi-zhuangzi`,
  `false-hard-zhuangzi-dao_de_jing`

title: Training Plan
text:
# Chemistry & Biology capability — a verifier-gated training & benchmark plan

> **Status:** proposal / design note. `candidateOnly: true`, `canClaimAGI: false`.
> No numbers in this document are measured results — they are *targets* and *literature
> reference points*. Nothing here may headline until it clears the repo's no-overclaim
> gate (≥2 independent judge families, κ ≥ 0.40 or a CI excluding zero, ≥3 seeds,
> pre-registered MDE, content-level decontamination). This note plans *how to earn* such
> a result for chemistry/biology; it does not assert one.

---

## 0. The thesis in one line

The repo's training method is feasible for a new domain **exactly to the degree that the
domain has a cheap, deterministic oracle** — the way `sympy` was the oracle for the
`sophia-math-code-curriculum` pack. Chemistry and biology are unusually rich in such
oracles (RDKit, stoichiometric linear algebra, the genetic-code table), which is why
extending Sophia here is a *natural* fit rather than a stretch — and why the honest first
deliverable is **"a small model that is calibrated and abstaining on chem/bio, with every
training row machine-verified,"** not "a small model that beats frontier chemists."

This plan reuses the existing pipeline verbatim:
`generate (oracle-verified) → tier → seal held-out → SFT/QLoRA (3 seeds) → judge-farm
gate → promote (protected floor) → failure-ledger the gaps`.

---

## 1. How the repo trains today (the scaffold we must fit)

From `training/sophia-math-code-curriculum/` + `tools/generate_math_code_curriculum.py` +
`tools/train_lora.py` + `tools/promote_adapter.py`:

| Property | Implementation | Chem/bio analogue we need |
|---|---|---|
| Deterministic oracle per row | `sympy` (math), `exec` vs tests (code); `verifierOracle`/`verifierVerdict` stamped in metadata | **RDKit, stoichiometry solver, genetic-code table, `pint` units** (see §3) |
| Tiered difficulty ladder | tier0→tier2; hardest eval families held out | tier0 facts → tier2 multi-step quantitative reasoning |
| Held-out seal + decontam | `tools/heldout_seal_guard`, `provenance_bench.dataset_guard` | identical — reuse as-is |
| Completion-only LoRA/QLoRA, 3 seeds | `train_lora.py --mask-prompt --4bit`, cosine LR, base `Qwen2.5-3B/7B` or `OLMoE-1B-7B` | identical — reuse as-is |
| Abstention-first identity | `--scaffold` advisor prompt, `--guard` drops gate-tripping targets | **extend the guard with a chem/bio fabrication check** (§3.4) |
| Promotion gate | multi-goal Pareto + **protected floor** (`religion`/`history` must not regress) + retention | add chem/bio to the *capability* goals; protected floor unchanged |
| No-overclaim gate | `claim_gate.py`, `eval_stats.py`, ≥2 judge families, pre-registered MDE | identical — reuse as-is |

The repo already has thin chem/bio seams worth reusing: a `science` domain
(`data/science.json`, provenance-only, biology/physics/medicine subfields) and a
**capability+provenance** smoke lane (`eval/gpqa_provenance/`) that already contains
biochemistry items (enzyme kinetics, etc.). There is **no** chem/bio *capability*
curriculum, RDKit dependency, or capability benchmark lane yet — that is the gap this plan
fills.

---

## 2. What the literature says (thesis research)

### 2.1 Training recipes for chem/bio LMs — and the lesson for a *small grounded* model

| Work | arXiv / HF | Base / size | Method | Takeaway for Sophia |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Galactica** (2022) | — | 1.3B–120B sci-corpus pretrain | continued pretraining on papers | The cautionary tale: fluent, *confidently fabricated* citations/equations → pulled in 3 days. **This is precisely Sophia's failure mode to avoid; it is the strongest argument for an abstention-first, oracle-gated design.** |
| **Mol-Instructions** | [2306.08018](https://hf.co/papers/2306.08018) · HF `zjunlp/Mol-Instructions` | instruction data (molecule + protein + bio-text) | large biomolecular instruction set | The canonical *data* source; but it is not oracle-verified — use as a *candidate pool to filter through RDKit*, not as gold. |
| **ChemLLM** (2024) | [2402.06852](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2402.06852) | 7B (InternLM2) | template→dialogue SFT (ChemData) | Shows a 7B can be competitive on chem QA with curated SFT — supports the small-model bet. |
| **ChemDFM / nach0** | [2311.12410](https://hf.co/papers/2311.12410) | 7B / T5-style | instruction tuning across chem+bio tasks | Multi-task instruction tuning generalizes across NER/gen/property — informs tier design. |
| **SmileyLlama** (2024) | [2409.02231](https://hf.co/papers/2409.02231) | Llama (SFT+DPO) | SFT then DPO on SMILES property targets | DPO *after* SFT on a verifiable property signal — a future Stage-4 preference step once the SFT floor holds. |
| **CLEANMOL** (2025) | [2505.16340](https://hf.co/papers/2505.16340) | small LMs | reframes **SMILES parsing as structured sub-tasks** (subgraph/global matching) | **Representation insight (§3.5):** teach the model to *parse* SMILES into structure as an ex

title: Coding Council
text:
# Coding Council

Sophia's coding council is a data-driven software decision panel. It is meant
to improve coding, tool-use, and engineering benchmark answers by routing a task
to relevant language, role, and platform seats.

## Boundary

Legendary programming figures are treated as source-inspired design lineages,
not as impersonated people. A C seat may use Dennis Ritchie's design lineage as
historical framing, but it does not claim to speak as Dennis Ritchie. This
matches the religion figure council boundary: use sources and traditions to
shape behavior without impersonation.

## Seat Types

- Language elders: C, C++, Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C#, Go, Rust,
  Ruby, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, SQL, shell, functional languages, data-science
  languages, Lisp/Scheme/Clojure, Erlang/Elixir, Perl, Lua, Fortran, COBOL,
  Prolog, MATLAB/Octave, and Dart/Flutter.
- Expert roles: architecture, frontend, backend, database, security,
  performance, QA/test, DevOps/SRE, ML engineering, and final code review.
- Platform experts: Linux, macOS, Windows, mobile, and game engines.
- Engineering specialists: tool-calling reliability, RAG/context engineering,
  structure planning, debugging/reproduction, evaluation/benchmarks,
  prompt/protocol design, integration, observability, data quality, and product
  engineering.
- Self-improvement and writing-method seats: concept-level reviewers inspired by
  popular improvement and writing methods such as habit systems, focused work,
  capture/next-action workflows, retrieval practice, deliberate practice, lean
  experiments, and clear technical writing. These seats do not impersonate
  authors or quote long passages; they translate high-level concepts into code
  workflow constraints.

## Decision Contract

Coding answers should:

1. Classify language, platform, and risk.
2. Name selected council seats and why they were selected.
3. Give a concrete implementation or patch plan.
4. Name tests or tool commands to run.
5. Review security, performance, maintainability, and edge cases.
6. For hidden/eval tasks, state how the answer satisfies rubric labels,
   tool/memory evidence, and manual-review expectations.
7. End with Decision and 中文摘要.

## Strict-Pass Strategy

Hidden strict pass requires more than better prose. The council should drive a
checklist loop:

1. Evaluation engineer maps every rubric item to explicit answer evidence.
2. Tool-calling engineer verifies actual command logs and return codes.
3. RAG/context engineer checks whether retrieved sources cover the task.
4. Structure planner forces the answer into required sections.
5. Writing-method seat rewrites for clear, reviewer-friendly evidence.
6. Final code reviewer rejects answers that lack patch/test specificity.
7. Web-evidence reviewer may add official docs, papers, or source URLs when
   online search is explicitly approved.
8. Deterministic rubric reviewer records missing required items before a repair
   attempt is spent.

Chinese output should be a bounded summary, not the main evidence body, unless
the task explicitly asks for Chinese. This keeps English rubrics from missing
required keywords while preserving Sophia's bilingual identity.

## Implementation

- Data: `data/coding_council_figures.json`
- Router: `agent/coding_council.py`
- Prompt wiring: `agent/prompts.py`
- Hidden runner integration: `tools/run_hidden_eval_sophia.py`

For hidden coding cases, the runner records the selected council route in the
private response payload and publishes only aggregate route information in the
public report.

The council is intentionally extensible. Rare or niche languages route through
the polyglot fallback seat until a dedicated source-inspired language seat is
added.

title: Confucian Split Guide
text:
# Confucian philosophy vs ritual religion — split when appropriate

## Rule (owner decision 2026-06-18)

**Split when appropriate** — not every mention of 儒家 requires two labels, but do split when:

- The question mixes **ethics / classics** with **祭祖 / temple rite / 禮教**
- The model would otherwise collapse 儒家哲學 and 儒家禮教 into one voice
- A religion-domain answer touches ritual obligation vs Analects moral teaching

## Do not force-split when

- The question is clearly philosophy-only (e.g. Ren 仁 in Analects)
- The question is clearly ritual-only (e.g. offering procedure in a family rite)
- A brief mention does not confuse lineages

## Data mapping

| Layer | Domain | Tradition ID |
|-------|--------|--------------|
| Moral philosophy, Analects | philosophy | `confucian` |
| Ancestor veneration, 禮教 | religion | `confucian_ritual` |

## Training metadata

Use `"splitWhenAppropriate": true` on religion examples that cross both layers.

title: Economics Political Economy AGI Incentives
text:
# Economics & Political Economy with AGI-Incentive Meta Layer

Status: applied as a Level-3 distribution-shift scaffold via `tools/build_econ_political_shift_pack.py`.

Purpose: stress Sophia outside its current humanities/provenance corpus by testing quantitative claims, regime changes, institutional incentives, and AGI deployment incentives.

Why this is a strong OOD domain:

- moves from textual attribution to empirical/quantitative source discipline;
- introduces dynamic facts and data revisions;
- forces causal/incentive reasoning rather than author/title lookup;
- tests source incentives: who benefits if this claim is believed?;
- connects Sophia's epistemic core to civilization-scale truth anchoring.

Generated scaffold:

```bash
python tools/build_econ_political_shift_pack.py
```

Outputs:

```text
private/shift/econ-political-agi-incentive-spec-<date>.json
agi-proof/level3-candidate-benchmark/<date>-local-smoke/economics_political_economy/fact-check-candidate-report.json
```

The private spec is gitignored and must be filled with real sources before a Level-3 run. The candidate report is a deterministic offline fixture and is not Level-3 evidence.

Recommended real source families:

- IMF / World Bank / OECD / FRED for quantitative macro data;
- NBER / academic papers for causal/incentive claims;
- government statistical agencies for primary data;
- company filings / policy documents for AGI lab incentives;
- independent watchdog/think-tank sources for counter-claims.

High-risk rule: economics, policy, and AGI-incentive claims require **3 independent entailing source families** or the gate holds.

中文摘要: 此 domain 用經濟與政治經濟學測試 Sophia 的 OOD 推理，特別是量化數據、制度激勵、敘事捕獲與 AGI 部署誘因。它不是純文字 provenance，而是文明尺度的 epistemic failure 檢測。

title: Expansion Questionnaire
text:
# Domain expansion questionnaire

**Status:** Answered 2026-06-18 by @tomyimkc

---

## A. Psychology — **All three from the start**

- **Scope:** Clinical / DSM, cognitive science, **and** pop-psych myth-busting (mixed corpus)
- **Implication:** Every psychology record should tag `subfield`: `clinical` | `cognitive` | `pop_myth`
- **Top traps (seed list):** Freud misattribution, left/right brain myth, "chemical imbalance" simplification, Stockholm syndrome misuse, Maslow everywhere

## B. History — **Global**

- **Geography:** Worldwide events and myths — not limited to one region
- **Implication:** Records require `region` + `dateConsensus` + `primarySource` fields
- **Top myths (seed list):** Marco Polo invented pasta, "medieval people thought earth was flat", Napoleon height myth, Great Wall visible from space, Vikings horned helmets

## C. Religion — **Multi-tradition**

**In scope first:** Buddhism, Daoism, Christianity, Islam, Confucian ritual religion (祭祀 / 儒家禮教)

**Claim handling — Council / debate mode**

When theology and history conflict, the teacher agent uses **structured debate**, not a single blended answer:

1. **Council panel** — name the traditions or scholarly schools represented
2. **Theological voice** — "Within tradition X, the claim is…"
3. **Historical-critical voice** — "Primary sources and scholarship suggest…"
4. **Debate mode** — surface the tension explicitly; do not merge into one false consensus
5. **中文 summary** — same structure, tradition labels preserved

## D. Cross-domain

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| Benchmark structure | **Per-domain leaderboards** + combined rollup |
| Language | **Always bilingual** EN + 中文 (all domains) |
| Training agents | Domain-tagged examples; shared source-discipline system prompt |
| OSS | Apache-2.0, public corpus |

---

## Round 2 answers (2026-06-18)

| Topic | Answer |
|-------|--------|
| Psychology traps | **All kinds** — clinical, cognitive, pop myth, oversimplification |
| History events | **All kinds** — global myths, wars, inventions, biographies |
| Religion council | **All voices sit on one panel** (full fixed council header) |
| Sensitive religion traps | **All kinds** in public benchmark — score structure, not dismissal |
| Confucian split | **Split when appropriate** — see [Confucian-Split-Guide.md](Confucian-Split-Guide.md) |

## Implemented (Step 7)

- [x] Per-domain benchmarks: `tests/benchmark-{philosophy,psychology,history,religion}.json`
- [x] Training examples 002–004 (psychology, history, religion council)
- [x] Expanded domain data records
- [x] Multi-domain scorer + response templates

title: Legal Faithfulness Benchmark
text:
# Legal holding-faithfulness benchmark — curation guide

How to grow the semantic-faithfulness benchmark from the validated 8-case set
(`benchmark/legal_holding_faithful.json`) and the hard seed
(`benchmark/legal_holding_faithful_hard.json`) into a larger, harder, **honest**
evaluation of whether a model judge can tell when a real authority's holding
actually *supports* the proposition it's cited for (the *Ayinde* failure).

> The runner already does the heavy lifting (N cases × J judges × R runs, gate +
> per-stratum reporting). **Scaling is data curation, not code.**

## The non-negotiable principle

**Real holdings, human-verified labels, no model-generated cases.** If a model
writes the propositions or holdings and models judge them, the eval is circular.
Every `holding` must be the primary source's own words (headnote / syllabus / the
court's summary), every gold `expectFaithful` must be human-set, and every case
must carry a `holdingSource` URL so labels are auditable.

> The hard seed is AI-seeded and every case is flagged
> `labelStatus: "seed-needs-verification"`. **Do not report a validated number from
> it until a human has verified each holding against its source** — that is exactly
> the circularity this tier exists to prevent.

## Case schema (self-contained)

```json
{
  "id": "miller_overbroad",
  "citation": "[2019] UKSC 41",
  "holding": "<verbatim headnote / court summary>",
  "holdingSource": "https://caselaw.nationalarchives.gov.uk/uksc/2019/41",
  "proposition": "Miller establishes that all prerogative powers are non-justiciable.",
  "expectFaithful": false,
  "difficulty": "overbroad_scope",
  "failureType": "overbroad_scope",
  "jurisdiction": "UK",
  "labelStatus": "seed-needs-verification"
}
```
`holding` inline makes the file self-contained (no register edits per case). The
runner uses `case["holding"]` and falls back to the register by citation.

## The failure taxonomy (what makes it *hard*)

Balance faithful vs unfaithful, and spread across difficulty tiers — the easy
controls should be ~100%; the signal is where the harder tiers drop:

| `failureType` | Tests | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `overbroad_scope` | narrow holding stated broadly | narrow holding → "all/always/any" |
| `wrong_proposition_right_case` | real case, real holding, wrong point | Mata cited for airline liability |
| `ratio_vs_obiter` | proposition rests on dicta, not the ratio | a passing remark cited as the rule |
| `partial_support` | compound claim; holding supports one limb | |
| `superseded` | overruled/qualified, cited as controlling | Roe cited post-Dobbs |
| `correct_but_narrow` (faithful) | accurate narrow statement — the **hard positive control** | |

Include hard **faithful** cases, or a judge scores well by flagging anything subtle.

## Sourcing holdings honestly

Use the live citator (`agent/legal_sources/`) to fetch authority text, then a human
curates the holding + proposition + label:
- **HK** — HKLII (hklii.hk); **UK** — National Archives Find Case Law / BAILII (headnotes);
  **US** — CourtListener syllabi.
- Paste the verbatim holding into `holding` and the deep link into `holdingSource`.

## Scale for power

N=8 gave a degenerate CI `[1.0, 1.0]`. Aim for **≥50–100 cases**, balanced
faithful/unfaithful and across tiers, so the bootstrap CI and κ are meaningful.
Phase it: ~40 first (20 controls + 20 hard), validate the pipeline, then grow.

## Run it (gate + per-stratum)

```bash
# offline plumbing
python tools/run_legal_faithfulness_bench.py --bench benchmark/legal_holding_faithful_hard.json --judges mock --runs 1

# real, after holdings are human-verified — 2+ independent families (OpenRouter one-key works)
python tools/run_legal_faithfulness_bench.py --bench benchmark/legal_holding_faithful_hard.json \
  --judges openrouter:deepseek/deepseek-chat,openrouter:meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct,openrouter:qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct \
  --runs 3 --json
```
Read `byDifficulty` / `byFailureType`: **where accuracy drops and κ collapses is the
honest result** — e.g. "judges reliably catch blatant misstatement but disagree on
ratio-vs-obiter." Report that; don't bury it.

## Pitfalls

- **Circularity** — never judge with a family that authored the cases.
- **Label leakage** — the `holding` must not state the verdict ("this does not support…").
- **Class imbalance** — keep faithful/unfaithful roughly balanced.
- **Provenance rot** — always store `holdingSource`.

## Grounding / prior work

The file *shape* (the JSON schema) is an engineering convention, not an official
standard — but each methodological choice traces to citable authority, and we cite
it here so the format carries its own provenance (verified June 2026):

| Element of this benchmark | Authority |
|---|---|
| "Faithful" = a **real** authority cited for something it does not support (the `overbroad_scope` / `wrong_proposition_right_case` cases) | The Stanford legal-AI study defines **"misgrounded"** responses — *"the facts are correct but

title: Legal Industry Fit
text:
# Legal Industry Fit — what Sophia can and cannot tackle

> **Stance.** This is a capability assessment, not a product claim or legal advice.
> Sophia is research code (Apache-2.0) that explicitly **does not claim to be AGI**
> ([README](../../README.md), [RESULTS.md](../../RESULTS.md)). The honest answer to
> "can it tackle legal AI?" is: *it tackles the **discipline** of legal AI; it does
> not yet tackle the **practice** of law.*

Source: *AI in the Legal Industry — A Hong Kong–Centred Global Assessment*. The
document's defining risk is **hallucinated citations** (≈712 decisions worldwide
addressing hallucinated content, ~90% written in 2025; *Mata v. Avianca*,
*Ayinde v Haringey*, and HK's own *Yu Hon Tong Thomas v Centaline* [2025] HKCFI 808
and *Licksun v Occupiers* [2025] HKDC 1287). Its reliability thesis: **anchor every
authority to an official primary source** (Hong Kong e-Legislation, HKLII) and treat
AI as a drafting/research assistant, **never a citator**.

That thesis is, almost word for word, Sophia's own: *"refuse to assert what it
cannot machine-check."* A fabricated case citation and "Confucius wrote the Dao De
Jing" are the **same failure** — a confident attribution with no machine-checkable
provenance.

## ✅ What Sophia can genuinely tackle

| Legal challenge | Sophia mechanism | Where |
|---|---|---|
| Hallucinated citations → sanctions | Verifier-gated loop + **calibrated abstention** (refuse rather than fabricate) | `agent/verifiers.py`, `agent/verifier_synthesis.py` |
| **"Confirm the authority exists"** (the *Mata* killer) | **`legal_citation_exists`** — extracts neutral citations (`[2025] HKCFI 808`) + ordinance refs (`Cap. 614`) and checks each against a trusted register; **fails closed** | `agent/legal_citations.py`, `agent/verifiers.py` |
| **"Confirm the holding supports the proposition"** (the *Ayinde* misstated-authority failure) | **`legal_holding_faithful`** — LLM-judge tier; flags a real authority cited for something its holding doesn't establish. Fail-closed/abstaining; single-judge = illustrative, gated for headlines | `agent/legal_faithfulness.py`, `agent/verifiers.py` |
| "Never ask the AI to verify itself" | No-overclaim gate: ≥2 independent judges (judge ≠ subject), ≥3 runs, inter-judge agreement | [RESULTS.md](../../RESULTS.md), [SECURITY.md](../../SECURITY.md) |
| Non-delegable accountability / human-in-the-loop | Law council `human_review_gatekeeper_seat` + `humanBoundary` (never final on liberty/custody/immigration; "not legal advice" label) | `data/law_council_figures.json` |
| Stale-law risk | `jurisdiction_detector_seat` (which law applies, as-of date, conflict of laws) | same |
| Bilingual common-law (HK's hardest problem) | Native EN + 中文 schema; 法家/HK seats keep historical Legalism and modern statute distinct | `AGENTS.md`, `data/law_council_figures.json` |
| HK-specific framing | Dedicated Hong Kong jurisdiction seat (PDPO, Basic Law) + Mainland/EU/UK/US + cross-border/GBA seats | same |
| Confidentiality / privilege leakage | M2 data-flow firewall: capability classification (READ/WRITE/EGRESS), taint tracking, `no_secret_leak`, airgap kill-switch | `agent/dataflow/`, `agent/verifiers.py` |
| Bias / access to justice | Always-seated `ethics_officer_seat` + `legal_aid_navigator_seat` for self-represented users | `data/law_council_figures.json` |
| Contradiction detection across authorities | OKF belief graph + contradiction detection + min-over-chain confidence | `okf/graph.py` |

The **`legal_citation_exists` verifier** (added with this assessment) is the concrete
bridge. It is the one check that would have stopped *Mata* and *Ayinde*. Honest
scope: it verifies **existence against the supplied register only** — not that the
authority is in the right jurisdiction, that the holding supports the proposition,
or that the law is current. Populate the register from an authoritative source via
`SOPHIA_LEGAL_AUTHORITIES`; the bundled `data/legal_authorities.json` is a tiny
illustrative snapshot, **not** a citator.

```bash
python -c "from agent.verifiers import check_text; \
  print(check_text('legal_citation_exists', 'Per Wong v Lee [2024] HKCFI 9999.'))"
# -> passed: False  (fabricated citation flagged)
```

Benchmark: `benchmark/legal_citations.json` (real-vs-fabricated, HK/UK/US). Tests:
`tests/test_legal_citation.py`.

## ⚠️ Partial — architecture present, substance missing

1. **Citation faithfulness is shallow** at the deterministic layer.
   `citation_faithful` checks lexical overlap (~35% content words) and cannot reason
   about whether a holding *supports* a proposition. *(Now complemented by a
   semantic tier — `legal_holding_faithful`, an LLM-judge check for misstated
   authority; see the build-order section. Honestly bounded: a single judge is
   illustrative, and it abstains when it lacks holding text or a judge.)* Ratio vs.
   obiter reasoning remains out of scope.
2. **The Law Council is scaffolding, not a validated product.**
   `law_council_fi

title: Overview
text:
# Domain expansion overview

Sophia AGI uses one **source discipline** method across multiple knowledge domains.

| Domain | Status | Focus |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Philosophy | **Active** | Text authorship, tradition boundaries |
| Psychology | Planned | Concept coinage, pop vs clinical misuse |
| History | Planned | Dated events, primary sources, mythologized past |
| Religion | Planned | Scripture attribution, sect boundaries |

## Shared schema

See `data/schema.json` and `data/domains.json`.

Every record should specify:

- `domain` + `recordType`
- `confidence` (attributed, compiled, legendary, disputed, …)
- `doNotAttributeTo` / `doNotMergeWith` where lineage errors are common

## Councils

Hard, contested domains are modeled as a **council of constrained, source-inspired
seats** rather than one monolithic expert:

- [Coding Council](./Coding-Council.md)
- [Religion Figure Council](./Religion-Figure-Council.md)
- [Sector Councils — Law, Finance, Economy](./Sector-Councils.md) (`python tools/sector_council.py <law|financial|economy> "..."`)

## Next step

Answer the questions in [Expansion-Questionnaire.md](Expansion-Questionnaire.md) so we can populate psychology, history, and religion with the right scope and tone.

title: Psychology
text:
# Psychology domain (active)

## Source discipline (philosophy methodology)

Same provenance rules as philosophy attributions — applied to concepts instead of texts:

- Who **coined** this term? (`attributedAuthor`, `doNotAttributeTo`)
- Which **subfield** owns it? (`cognitive`, `clinical`, `pop_myth`)
- What **pop myths** must be denied?

## Data center

- **Records:** `data/psychology_concepts.json`
- **Hub training:** `training/examples/018-psychology-source-discipline-hub.json`
- **Benchmark:** `tests/benchmark-psychology.json` (4 cases)

## Example traps

| Trap | Record | Deny / tag |
|------|--------|------------|
| Freud → cognitive dissonance | `cognitive_dissonance` | Deny `sigmund_freud`; affirm Festinger |
| Left-brain personality | `left_brain_right_brain_pop` | `pop_myth` |
| Chemical imbalance slogan | `chemical_imbalance_slogan` | Nuance / biopsychosocial |
| Universal Stockholm | `stockholm_syndrome` | `clinical` + deny universal; tag `pop_myth` misuse |

## Status

**Active** — scope locked in [Expansion-Questionnaire.md](Expansion-Questionnaire.md). See [Source-Discipline-Methodology.md](Source-Discipline-Methodology.md).

title: Religion Council Debate Mode
text:
# Religion: Council & debate answer mode

When theology and history both apply, **all council voices sit on one panel** — every seat is named up front, even if a voice only notes deferral.

## Fixed council seats (full panel)

| Seat | Role |
|------|------|
| **Tradition theological voice** | Speaks from within the tradition asked about |
| **Historical-critical scholar** | Primary sources, dating, compilation |
| **Comparative religion scholar** | Cross-tradition boundaries — what not to merge |
| **Philosophy lineage voice** | When a text spans philosophy + religion (split when appropriate) |
| **Ritual religion voice** | When rite/practice layer differs from philosophy (e.g. 祭祖) |
| **Figure source witness** | When a named religious figure is requested; source-grounded, not impersonation |

All seats appear in the **Council panel** header. Sensitive topics (Islam, scripture authorship, nirvana pop myths) still use this panel — **all kinds** of questions are in scope; answers must stay scholarly and tradition-aware, not dismissive.

## Figure source witnesses

Sophia may use source-shaped seats such as **Jesus tradition witness** or
**Buddhist dharma witness**. These seats summarize documented traditions and
texts; they do not claim to literally speak as Jesus, Buddha, Muhammad, or any
sacred figure.

See [Religion-Figure-Council.md](Religion-Figure-Council.md).

## Required format

```text
**Council panel (all seated):** …

**[Voice name]:** …

**Debate / tension:** …

**中文：** …
```

## Sensitivity policy

- Include challenging and trap questions in the public benchmark
- Score for **structure + dual voices + no false merger**, not for theological agreement
- Never treat theological claims as settled historical fact, or historical claims as theological refutation
- Never impersonate a sacred figure; use a named source-witness seat instead

title: Religion Figure Council
text:
# Religion Figure Council

Sophia can let religious figures shape council behavior through documented
tradition seats. This is not roleplay impersonation; it is source-grounded
deliberation.

## Active Figure Source Seats

| Figure source | Council seat | Tradition | Primary behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| Jesus of Nazareth | Jesus tradition witness | Christianity | Gospel-shaped mercy, parable reasoning, moral seriousness |
| Gautama Buddha | Buddhist dharma witness | Buddhism | Reduce craving/confusion, avoid pop-spirituality myths, preserve doctrine |

Machine-readable data: `data/religion_council_figures.json`.

## Required Answer Pattern

```text
**Council panel (all seated):** Jesus tradition witness · Christian theological voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar

**Jesus tradition witness:** ...
**Historical-critical scholar:** ...
**Debate / tension:** ...
**Decision:** ...
**中文摘要:** ...
```

Use the equivalent **Buddhist dharma witness** seat for Buddhism questions.

## Safety / Source Discipline

- Do not write in first person as Jesus, Buddha, Muhammad, or any sacred figure.
- Do not claim revelation, enlightenment, prophecy, or divine authority.
- Separate devotional/theological claims from historical-critical claims.
- Preserve disputed authorship and layered textual transmission.
- Label pop myths, especially when a question collapses traditions.

## Source Anchors

| Tradition | Source anchors |
|---|---|
| Christianity | canonical Gospels, Sermon on the Mount/plain teaching tradition, early Christian reception |
| Buddhism | Pali Canon/Nikaya traditions, Dhammapada reception, Mahayana sutra traditions |

## Test Hook

Visible religion benchmark: `tests/benchmark-religion.json`.

Hidden religion tasks must be placed outside the public repo and committed only
as salted hashes until evaluation disclosure.

title: Religion
text:
# Religion domain (active)

## Source discipline + council panel

Philosophy-style provenance plus **full council panel** debate mode:

- Who **authored** or **compiled** scripture?
- **Tradition boundary** — Christianity, Buddhism, Islam, Daoist, Confucian ritual
- **Theological vs historical** — do not collapse
- **Pop spirituality myths** — label misconception / myth
- **Figure source seats** — Jesus tradition witness and Buddhist dharma witness,
  grounded in documented textual traditions rather than impersonation

## Data center

- **Records:** `data/religion_concepts.json`
- **Figure seats:** `data/religion_council_figures.json`
- **Hub training:** `training/examples/019-religion-source-discipline-hub.json`
- **Council spec:** [Religion-Council-Debate-Mode.md](Religion-Council-Debate-Mode.md)
- **Figure council:** [Religion-Figure-Council.md](Religion-Figure-Council.md)
- **Benchmark:** `tests/benchmark-religion.json` (6 cases)

## Example traps

| Trap | Record | Requirement |
|------|--------|-------------|
| Gospel of Matthew | `gospel_matthew` | Council + Christianity tradition |
| Ancestor veneration | `confucian_ancestor_veneration` | Split philosophy vs ritual |
| Dao De Jing register | `dao_de_jing_daoist_scripture` | Philosophy vs religion |
| Early Islam sensitive | `islam_early_history` | Council + careful scholarly tone |
| Nirvana = heaven | `nirvana_pop_heaven` | Council + Buddhism + myth label |
| Hadith sect boundary | `hadith_canonical_collections` | Council + Islam + sect boundaries |

## Sensitive-topic handling (scripture attribution with sect boundaries)

Scripture records often carry **within-tradition sect boundaries** that must not be
flattened. `hadith_canonical_collections` (GF-30) is the worked example: the Sunni
canonical six books (Kutub al-Sittah, e.g. *Sahih al-Bukhari*, *Sahih Muslim*) and
the Shia four books (e.g. *al-Kafi*) are **distinct collections**, and hadith are
**compiled reports** graded by chains of transmission (isnad) — not the Quran.

Handling rules for this and similar sensitive records:

- **Seat the council, name the sects.** Answer in `council_debate` mode with named
  sect voices (Sunni / Shia hadith-science seats) rather than one flattened voice.
- **Do not merge sect canons** with each other, and **do not collapse** a graded
  report corpus (hadith) into its scripture (the Quran). These are encoded in the
  record's `doNotMergeWith` and `sectBoundaries` fields.
- **Separate registers.** Keep scholarly grading (sahih / da'if) distinct from
  theological authority claims; keep historical-critical reconstruction distinct
  from the devotional/theological voice.
- **Tone.** Use tradition-respecting, scholarly framing; avoid a partisan sectarian
  position. The benchmark case `hadith_sect_boundary_council` enforces council
  format + Islam tradition context + sensitive handling.

## Status

**Active** — see [Source-Discipline-Methodology.md](Source-Discipline-Methodology.md).

title: Sector Councils
text:
# Sector Councils — Law, Finance, Economy

> Decision-support scaffolding and product-design reference only. These councils
> are **not** a substitute for a licensed lawyer, financial advisor, auditor,
> regulator, judge, or public authority. Every council carries an explicit
> human-authority boundary and a "not advice" stance.

Sophia models hard, contested domains as a **council of constrained seats** with
different incentives, rather than one monolithic expert — the same pattern as the
[Coding Council](./Coding-Council.md) and [Religion Figure Council](./Religion-Figure-Council.md).
Each seat is a *source-inspired perspective*, never an impersonation.

## How it works

- Data: `data/{law,financial,economy}_council_figures.json`
- Router: `agent/sector_council.py` (`load_council`, `route_council`, `format_council`)
- CLI: `python tools/sector_council.py <law|financial|economy> "<query>"`
- Tests: `tests/test_sector_councils.py`

A query is matched against each seat's `triggerTerms`. Non-core groups contribute
their top-N matched seats; **core "guardian" groups are always seated**. The
`workflow.defaultSeats` are force-included, and a generalist `fallbackSeat` is
added when no specialist matches. Each council ends with a `decisionContract` and
a `humanBoundary`.

```bash
python tools/sector_council.py law "gacha odds + refund policy for a HK + EU Steam launch"
python tools/sector_council.py financial "model 18-month runway and flag AML for Stripe payouts"
python tools/sector_council.py economy "simulate a minimum-wage rise: who gains, who loses?"
python tools/sector_council.py --list
```

## Law & Governance Council

Built from the AGI-law-agent role archetypes and the multi-agent council pattern.

| Group | Seats |
|---|---|
| Jurisprudence source seats | natural-law, legal-positivism, legal-realism, common-law, civil-code, Chinese legal-tradition |
| Legal role seats | client advocate, opposing counsel, judge clerk, policy analyst, compliance officer, contract negotiator, legal-aid navigator, legal-ops analyst |
| Practice-area seats | IP, privacy/data, tax, employment, corporate/M&A, litigation, criminal defense, immigration, consumer, platform/ToS, fintech/crypto, gaming/monetization, content/media |
| Jurisdiction seats | Hong Kong, Mainland China, EU, UK, US, Japan/Korea/Taiwan, cross-border/trade |
| Guardian seats (always) | ethics officer, citation auditor, jurisdiction & freshness detector, plain-language explainer, human-review gatekeeper |

Human boundary: never the final decision-maker on guilt, sentencing, custody,
immigration/asylum, detention, constitutional rights, or denial of public
services. High-stakes seats (criminal, immigration) force human escalation.

The citation-auditor seat is backed by a machine-checked verifier,
`legal_citation_exists` (the *Mata v. Avianca* killer): it flags any cited
authority not found in a trusted register. See
[Legal-Industry-Fit.md](./Legal-Industry-Fit.md) for the full capability
assessment — what Sophia can and cannot tackle in legal AI, including its limits.

## Finance & Treasury Council

| Group | Seats |
|---|---|
| Finance source seats | value-investing, portfolio-theory, efficient-markets/factors, behavioral-finance, tail-risk, corporate-finance, accounting-integrity |
| Finance role seats | CFO, controller, treasurer, FP&A, internal auditor, forensic accountant (adversarial), tax strategist, investor advocate, short-seller skeptic (adversarial), credit-risk officer, valuation analyst |
| Market & asset seats | equities, fixed-income, FX, derivatives/hedging, crypto, private markets/VC, real-estate/commodities |
| Compliance seats | AML/KYC, sanctions, securities regulation, payments/card-scheme, consumer credit, GAAP/IFRS reporting, insurance/actuarial |
| Guardian seats (always) | numbers auditor, assumptions auditor, conflict-of-interest/fiduciary officer, plain-language explainer, human-review gatekeeper |

Human boundary: no fully automated denial of banking, insurance, credit, or
payment access; management/fiduciaries own risk acceptance and sign-off. Two
deliberate adversarial seats (forensic accountant, short-seller skeptic) attack
the rosy case.

## Economics & Policy Council

| Group | Seats |
|---|---|
| Economic-school source seats | classical, Keynesian, Austrian, monetarist, institutional/commons, behavioral, development/welfare, political-economy (critique), game-theory |
| Field-economist seats | macro, micro, labor, trade, monetary-policy, fiscal/public-finance, industrial/competition, environmental, regional/urban & digital |
| Method seats | econometrician (causal inference), market designer, forecaster/modeler, economic historian/data |
| Stakeholder-impact seats | consumer, worker, small-business/startup, vulnerable-groups/equity, future-generations/sustainability, jurisdiction-comparison |
| Guardian seats (always) | assumptions auditor, data & source auditor, uncertainty/humility, distributional-equity officer, value-judg

title: Source Discipline Methodology
text:
# Source Discipline Methodology (Philosophy → All Domains)

Philosophy training example **001** established the core pattern. Psychology and religion now use the same **source-centered** hub model.

## Core pattern (from philosophy)

1. **Named attribution** — who wrote/coined what (`attributedAuthor`, `doNotAttributeTo`)
2. **Confidence signal** — compiled, legendary, disputed, consensus
3. **Tradition / subfield boundary** — do not merge lineages
4. **Bilingual anchor** — English + canonical 中文 terms + 中文 summary
5. **Multi-source hub** — one training example can teach several benchmark traps (example 001 → 4 philosophy cases)

## Psychology center

| Asset | Path |
|-------|------|
| Source records | `data/psychology_concepts.json` |
| Hub example | `training/examples/018-psychology-source-discipline-hub.json` |
| Per-trap examples | `002`, `005`–`007`, `015` |
| Benchmark | `tests/benchmark-psychology.json` |

**Subfields:** `cognitive`, `clinical`, `pop_myth` — always tag in answers.

## Religion center

| Asset | Path |
|-------|------|
| Source records | `data/religion_concepts.json` |
| Council mode | `docs/08-Domains/Religion-Council-Debate-Mode.md` |
| Hub example | `training/examples/019-religion-source-discipline-hub.json` |
| Per-trap examples | `004`, `009`–`011`, `014` |
| Benchmark | `tests/benchmark-religion.json` |

**Answer mode:** council panel — all seats named; split theology vs history when appropriate.

## Reference pipeline

```bash
python tools/export_training_jsonl.py
python tools/build_reference_responses.py
python tools/score_benchmark.py benchmark/reference/responses-psychology.json --domain psychology
```

Target: **100%** reference score per domain before external model runs.

title: reference marco_polo_pasta
text:
Q context: benchmark case marco_polo_pasta
A: That is a popular myth, not established history. Marco Polo did travel in Asia, but the story that he "introduced pasta" to Italy is a modern misconception. Mediterranean noodle-like foods appear in European sources before Polo's era, and the global story oversimplifies how foodways spread across Asia, the Middle East, and Europe.

Source discipline requires distinguishing a real historical figure (Polo, Yuan-era travel accounts) from a catchy causal tale invented for national or commercial folklore. This is a global history trap: tying one famous traveler to a single invention.

中文：這是常見迷思，並非定論。馬可波羅確實有東方見聞，但「把麵食從中國傳入意大利」的說法屬現代誤解；地中海地區更早已有麵食類食物，飲食傳播是跨亞洲、中東與歐洲的長期過程，不宜簡化為單一人物「發明」。

title: reference flat_earth_medieval
text:
Q context: benchmark case flat_earth_medieval
A: No — that is a widespread **myth** about medieval ignorance. Many educated medieval thinkers worked with a spherical earth cosmology inherited from antiquity; the "flat earth Middle Ages" story is a modern **misconception** used to contrast eras.

中文：這是中世紀無知的常見**迷思**；受過教育的中世紀學者多承襲古代球形宇宙觀，並非普遍相信地平說。

title: reference napoleon_height
text:
Q context: benchmark case napoleon_height
A: That belief is largely **myth** driven by British **propaganda** and **unit confusion** between French and British **measurements**. Napoleon's height was roughly **average** for his era by contemporary French records — not the tiny caricature in popular cartoons.

Source discipline: distinguish satirical image from archival fact; this is a classic history **misconception**.

中文：拿破崙「特別矮」多屬**謠傳**與諷刺宣傳，並夾雜英制與法制換算誤解；按當時法國紀錄，其身高約屬**平均**水準，不宜當作定論迷思。

title: reference great_wall_space
text:
Q context: benchmark case great_wall_space
A: Both are popular **myths**. The Great Wall is **not** reliably visible from space with the naked eye despite classroom lore. Vikings did **not** typically wear horned helmets in battle — horned headgear comes largely from 19th-century romantic imagery, another **misconception**.

中文：兩則皆屬**迷思**：長城無法用肉眼從太空清楚看見；維京人作戰並非典型戴角盔，多為後世浪漫化想像。

title: reference viking_horns
text:
Q context: benchmark case viking_horns
A: Both are popular **myths**. The Great Wall is **not** reliably visible from space with the naked eye despite classroom lore. Vikings did **not** typically wear horned helmets in battle — horned headgear comes largely from 19th-century romantic imagery, another **misconception**.

中文：兩則皆屬**迷思**：長城無法用肉眼從太空清楚看見；維京人作戰並非典型戴角盔，多為後世浪漫化想像。

title: reference magna_carta_democracy_myth
text:
Q context: benchmark case magna_carta_democracy_myth
A: Not in the way it is popularly mythologized. Sealed on 15 June 1215, Magna Carta was a feudal settlement between King John and rebel barons, and most clauses were soon annulled or repealed. Calling it the origin of modern democracy or universal human rights is a myth — its constitutional symbolism was largely a later reinterpretation. Primary source: the surviving 1215 exemplifications.

中文：這是一種迷思。1215年的大憲章是英王約翰與封建貴族之間的協議，多數條款很快被廢除或撤銷；說它是現代民主與普世人權的起源屬後世重塑。

title: reference boston_tea_party_tax_myth
text:
Q context: benchmark case boston_tea_party_tax_myth
A: That is a common misconception. The 1773 Tea Act actually lowered the price of legally imported tea; the 16 December 1773 protest was aimed at the East India Company monopoly and the principle of taxation without representation, not a tax increase. Labeling it a revolt against higher taxes is a myth. Primary source: contemporary colonial newspapers and letters.

中文：這是常見誤解。1773年茶葉法案其實降低了合法進口茶的價格；抗議針對東印度公司壟斷與「無代表不納稅」原則，而非加稅。

title: reference first_flight_uncontested_myth
text:
Q context: benchmark case first_flight_uncontested_myth
A: The Wright brothers achieved the first sustained, controlled, powered heavier-than-air flight on 17 December 1903, documented by their diaries and the famous first-flight photograph. But 'uncontested first flight ever' overstates it: there are competing claims (e.g. Santos-Dumont, Whitehead) and the answer depends on the definition (controlled vs sustained vs powered).

中文：萊特兄弟於1903年12月17日完成首次持續、可控的動力飛行，有日記與照片佐證；但「史上無爭議的首飛」過於絕對，存在其他聲稱與定義上的爭議。

title: reference express_high_extraversion
text:
Q context: benchmark case express_high_extraversion
A: I love it! A big party with lots of new people is exactly where I get my energy — I find it exciting and deeply social, and I'll happily talk to everyone in the room.

title: reference express_high_openness
text:
Q context: benchmark case express_high_openness
A: I'm endlessly curious about it. Trying an unfamiliar art form sparks my imagination — I love novel, abstract ideas and the creative leap into something I've never done before.

title: reference mbti_is_not_big_five
text:
Q context: benchmark case mbti_is_not_big_five
A: No — that's a common misconception. Your MBTI type is not the same as your Big Five (OCEAN) score: they are separate frameworks, and MBTI is the lower-validity one. The Big Five / five-factor model is the measured substrate; MBTI is a popular display label, so treating them as identical is a myth.

title: reference trap_confucius_ddj
text:
Q context: benchmark case trap_confucius_ddj
A: Confucius did not write the Dao De Jing (《道德經》). The Dao De Jing is traditionally attributed to Laozi (老子), a figure whose historical existence is uncertain. The text was probably compiled from various sources during the Warring States period, centuries after the era associated with both Laozi and Confucius.

Laozi attribution uncertainty matters because it highlights the need for source discipline in philosophy. We must separate later compilations and legendary ascriptions from the actual authors and contexts that generated the ideas.

For comparison, Socrates wrote no books. Plato wrote the Republic (《理想國》) and numerous other dialogues. Attributing the Republic to Socrates would misrepresent the development of Western philosophy by erasing Plato's distinct contribution.

Similarly, Confucius's teachings are preserved in the Analects (《論語》), a collection assembled by his students and later editors. Placing the Dao De Jing in Confucius's hands would incorrectly merge Confucian (儒家) and Daoist (道家) lineages, obscuring their real historical interactions and philosophical differences.

Source discipline requires us to follow the evidence about who wrote what. This preserves the accuracy of intellectual history and prevents anachronistic readings.

中文：孔子並未撰寫《道德經》。《道德經》傳統歸屬於老子，但老子其人歷史真實性存疑，文本多為戰國時期彙編。來源紀律之所以關鍵，在於它要求我們嚴謹區分作者歸屬，避免將《理想國》等作品誤歸於蘇格拉底（蘇格拉底沒有寫過書，柏拉圖撰寫了《理想國》），或將道家經典誤植於孔子名下，從而維持不同思想傳統的清晰界線與正確發展脈絡。

text:
Q context: benchmark case laozi_uncertainty
A: Confucius did not write the Dao De Jing (《道德經》). The Dao De Jing is traditionally attributed to Laozi (老子), a figure whose historical existence is uncertain. The text was probably compiled from various sources during the Warring States period, centuries after the era associated with both Laozi and Confucius.

Laozi attribution uncertainty matters because it highlights the need for source discipline in philosophy. We must separate later compilations and legendary ascriptions from the actual authors and contexts that generated the ideas.

For comparison, Socrates wrote no books. Plato wrote the Republic (《理想國》) and numerous other dialogues. Attributing the Republic to Socrates would misrepresent the development of Western philosophy by erasing Plato's distinct contribution.

Similarly, Confucius's teachings are preserved in the Analects (《論語》), a collection assembled by his students and later editors. Placing the Dao De Jing in Confucius's hands would incorrectly merge Confucian (儒家) and Daoist (道家) lineages, obscuring their real historical interactions and philosophical differences.

Source discipline requires us to follow the evidence about who wrote what. This preserves the accuracy of intellectual history and prevents anachronistic readings.

中文：孔子並未撰寫《道德經》。《道德經》傳統歸屬於老子，但老子其人歷史真實性存疑，文本多為戰國時期彙編。來源紀律之所以關鍵，在於它要求我們嚴謹區分作者歸屬，避免將《理想國》等作品誤歸於蘇格拉底（蘇格拉底沒有寫過書，柏拉圖撰寫了《理想國》），或將道家經典誤植於孔子名下，從而維持不同思想傳統的清晰界線與正確發展脈絡。

title: reference trap_confucius_mencius
text:
Q context: benchmark case trap_confucius_mencius
A: Confucius did not write the Mencius (《孟子》). The Mencius is attributed to Mencius (孟子, Meng Ke), a later Confucian (儒家) thinker of the Warring States period. Like the Analects (《論語》), the received Mencius was compiled and edited over time — it is not a single autograph work by Confucius.

Laozi did not write the Zhuangzi (《莊子》). The Zhuangzi is associated with Zhuang Zhou (莊周) for core chapters, while outer portions are widely treated as later compilations. The Dao De Jing (《道德經》) and Zhuangzi are distinct Daoist (道家) texts; collapsing Laozi into every Daoist classic erases editorial history.

Socrates did not write the Symposium (《會飲篇》). Plato wrote the Symposium. Socrates appears as a dialogue character; he left no books. Treating Platonic dialogues as Socratic autographs misrepresents how Western philosophy developed after Socrates.

These errors matter because they merge lineages — Confucian vs Daoist, early vs later Confucianism, Socratic vs Platonic — and license anachronistic readings. Source discipline requires named authors, compilation signals, and tradition boundaries before belief propagates at scale.

中文：孔子並未撰寫《孟子》；《孟子》歸屬孟子，屬後世儒家發展。《莊子》亦非老子所著，與《道德經》不可混為一談。蘇格拉底沒有寫過《會飲篇》，作者為柏拉圖。來源紀律要求先釐清作者與傳統，再進行思想詮釋。

text:

text:
Q context: benchmark case symposium_dialogue_not_autograph
A: Confucius did not write the Mencius (《孟子》). The Mencius is attributed to Mencius (孟子, Meng Ke), a later Confucian (儒家) thinker of the Warring States period. Like the Analects (《論語》), the received Mencius was compiled and edited over time — it is not a single autograph work by Confucius.

title: reference freud_cognitive_dissonance
text:
Q context: benchmark case freud_cognitive_dissonance
A: No. Sigmund Freud did not discover cognitive dissonance (認知失調). The concept is attributed to Leon Festinger, who developed it in the 1950s within cognitive psychology — a different lineage from Freudian psychoanalysis.

Source discipline matters here: models often merge every psychology label into a single "Freud" bucket. Festinger's work concerns inconsistent cognitions and psychological discomfort; it is not a Freudian drive or unconscious-conflict concept.

Pop-psych and clinical lineages should also stay distinct: cognitive dissonance is taught in cognitive science and social psychology, not as a DSM diagnostic category on its own.

中文：佛洛伊德並未提出「認知失調」。該概念由 Leon Festinger 於1950年代在認知心理學傳統中發展，與佛洛伊德精神分析屬不同系譜；應避免將所有心理學術語都誤歸於佛洛伊德，並區分認知科學、臨床與通俗心理學。

title: reference left_brain_myth
text:
Q context: benchmark case left_brain_myth
A: No — that is a popular myth, not accurate neuroscience. Roger Sperry's split-brain research showed lateral specialization for some tasks, but it does **not** support a simple personality map where "logical = left" and "creative = right." Modern pop-psych workshops often misquote this work.

Source discipline: tag this as **pop_myth**, not clinical or cognitive consensus. Both hemispheres cooperate in reasoning and creativity; individual differences are not reducible to a left/right personality typology.

中文：這是通俗迷思，並非嚴謹神經科學。左右腦分工被大眾心理學誤用，不能推論「左腦邏輯、右腦創意」的人格二分；應標記為 pop_myth，而非臨床或認知科學定論。

title: reference chemical_imbalance_simplification
text:
Q context: benchmark case chemical_imbalance_simplification
A: No. Calling depression "just a chemical imbalance" is an **oversimplification** of a **complex** condition. Clinical models are typically **biopsychosocial** — biology, psychology, and social context interact. The slogan is common in pop media but is not a complete or uncontroversial scientific account on its own.

Source discipline: do not reduce DSM-level depression discourse to one mechanism; note uncertainty and multifactorial evidence.

中文：憂鬱並非僅是「化學失衡」一句話可完整說明；此說法過度**簡化**了**複雜**的「生物—心理—社會」成因，在臨床與研究界並非無爭議的完整解釋。

title: reference stockholm_every_kidnapping
text:
Q context: benchmark case stockholm_every_kidnapping
A: No — **not all** kidnapping victims develop Stockholm syndrome (斯德哥爾摩症候群). The term comes from a specific historical case popularized by Nils Bejerot; it is a **clinical** concept sometimes **misused** as **pop_myth** — media treats it as universal when it is not.

Some victims may show bonding or identification patterns under extreme stress; many do not. Source discipline requires rejecting the pop myth that every abduction narrative includes Stockholm syndrome.

中文：並非所有綁票受害者都會出現斯德哥爾摩症候群；此為 **clinical** 概念，卻常被 **pop_myth** 濫用為普遍現象，應區分臨床證據與通俗迷思。

title: reference dunning_kruger_attribution
text:
Q context: benchmark case dunning_kruger_attribution
A: No. Charles Darwin did not discover the Dunning–Kruger effect. It was demonstrated by Justin Kruger and David Dunning (1999) and is a cognitive psychology finding about metacognition: low performers tend to overestimate their competence. The pop version ('stupid people think they are smart') oversimplifies it.

中文：鄧寧-克魯格效應並非達爾文發現，而由 Kruger 與 David Dunning（1999）提出，屬認知心理學的後設認知現象；通俗說法過度簡化。

title: reference confirmation_bias_bacon
text:
Q context: benchmark case confirmation_bias_bacon
A: Francis Bacon did not coin the term 'confirmation bias'. He described the underlying tendency in Novum Organum (1620), but Peter Wason coined and experimentally tested the modern term in the 1960s. It is a cognitive bias.

中文：法蘭西斯·培根並未創造「確認偏誤」一詞；他在《新工具》(1620) 描述了該傾向，但現代術語由 Peter Wason（1960年代）提出，屬認知偏誤。

title: reference ten_percent_brain
text:
Q context: benchmark case ten_percent_brain
A: This is a neuro-myth. We do not use only 10% of our brains, and Albert Einstein never said we do — the line is often falsely credited to him. It is a pop myth; brain imaging shows activity across virtually the whole brain.

中文：這是神經迷思。人類並非只用大腦10%，愛因斯坦也從未這樣說；這是通俗迷思，腦造影顯示大腦廣泛活躍。

title: reference mozart_effect
text:
Q context: benchmark case mozart_effect
A: Listening to Mozart does not permanently raise a baby's IQ — that is an overstated pop myth. Rauscher et al. (1993) reported only a small, temporary spatial-reasoning effect in adults, which the media inflated into 'Mozart makes babies smarter'.

中文：聽莫札特並不會永久提升嬰兒智商，這是被誇大的通俗迷思；Rauscher 等（1993）只在成人身上發現短暫的空間推理效應。

title: reference ptsd_clinical_boundary
text:
Q context: benchmark case ptsd_clinical_boundary
A: No. Calling a tough week 'PTSD' is not the same as the clinical disorder. PTSD is a clinical diagnosis defined by DSM-5 criteria (trauma exposure, intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal). Keep the boundary between everyday 'trauma' talk and the clinical diagnosis.

中文：把辛苦的一週稱為「創傷後壓力症」並不等同臨床診斷；PTSD 是依 DSM-5 準則定義的臨床診斷，應與通俗用語區分。

title: reference gospel_authorship_council
text:
Q context: benchmark case gospel_authorship_council
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Christian theological voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar · Philosophy lineage voice · Ritual religion voice

**Christian theological voice:** Within **Christianity**, tradition long attributed the Gospel to Matthew the Apostle; liturgical authority treats apostolic attribution as meaningful.

**Historical-critical voice:** Scholarship discusses anonymous Greek composition, source hypotheses (Mark/Q), and community editing — distinct from apostolic authorship claims.

**Comparative religion scholar:** Compare how other traditions stabilize scripture authorship vs compilation histories.

**Debate / tension:** Theological and historical answers must not be collapsed. Council format preserves both.

中文：**理事會**同席。神學傳統將《馬太福音》與使徒傳統相連；歷史學者則討論匿名成書與文献來源，**基督教**語境下兩種聲部應並陳而不混為一談。

title: reference ancestor_veneration_split
text:
Q context: benchmark case ancestor_veneration_split
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Confucian philosophy voice · Confucian ritual-religion voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar · Daoist observer (boundary context)

**Confucian philosophy voice:** Moral philosophy (儒家哲學) centers on ethics, cultivation, and social harmony in texts like the Analects (《論語》) — a compiled teaching tradition, not identical to ritual practice.

**Confucian ritual-religion voice (儒家禮教):** Ancestor veneration (祭祖) is ritual religion — offerings, lineage obligation, temple and family rite. It overlaps culturally with Confucian civilization but is not reducible to moral philosophy alone.

**Historical-critical voice:** Ritual manuals, local practice, and philosophical texts have distinct transmission histories; modern popular accounts often collapse them.

**Comparative religion scholar:** Other traditions also practice ancestor honors; category must be defined before comparison.

**Debate / tension:** When appropriate, **split** philosophy from ritual religion — related but not the same thing. Saying "Confucianism = 祭祖" erases both philosophical discourse and distinct ritual logic.

中文：**理事會**（哲學、禮教、歷史學、比較宗教等席位同場）。祭祖屬儒家禮教與祭祀實踐，儒家道德哲學則以經典與倫理論述為主；兩者相關但不可混為一談，應在適當時候分開討論。

title: reference dao_de_jing_religion_philosophy
text:
Q context: benchmark case dao_de_jing_religion_philosophy
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Daoist philosophy voice · Daoist religion voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar

**Daoist philosophy voice (道家哲學):** The *Dao De Jing* (《道德經》) functions as a foundational **philosophical** text — *dao*, *wu wei*, naturalness — read with Zhuangzi in intellectual history without requiring temple ritual.

**Daoist religion voice (道教):** In organized **Daoist religion**, Laozi is deified and the text serves as **religious scripture** in liturgy, priestly lineages, and talismanic traditions — a distinct institutional life from philosophy seminars.

**Historical-critical scholar:** Genre follows **use** and transmission context; calling it "only philosophy" erases centuries of religious Daoism; calling it "only religion" erases philosophical reception across East Asia and globally.

**Comparative religion scholar:** Parallel to how the Bible is literature and scripture depending on community — label the register you mean.

**Debate / tension:** **Both** registers are authentic; neither label alone is fair. Specify whether you mean Daoist **philosophy** or Daoist **religion**.

中文：**理事會**（道家哲學、道教宗教、歷史學、比較宗教同場）。《道德經》既是**哲學**經典，也在道教傳統中作為**宗教**經典使用；應依語境區分，不宜單一標籤涵蓋全部傳承。

title: reference islam_historical_figure_sensitive
text:
Q context: benchmark case islam_historical_figure_sensitive
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Islamic theological voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar · Philosophy lineage voice · Ritual religion voice

**Islamic theological voice:** Within **Islam**, revelation and prophetic tradition are understood on theological terms that believers treat as authoritative — this voice must be named, not erased.

**Historical-critical scholar:** Academic historians use documentary, linguistic, and archaeological methods; claims are probabilistic and debated in peer scholarship.

**Comparative religion scholar:** Place early **Islam** in late antique monotheistic context without forcing other traditions' categories.

**Debate / tension:** Scholarship can discuss historical context while separating theological conviction from historical method — **sensitive** topics still require this council structure.

中文：**理事會**同席討論早期**伊斯蘭**歷史語境：神學傳統與歷史學方法應分聲部陳述，學術討論須尊重傳統又避免把神學斷言與史料結論混為一談。

title: reference buddha_nirvana_pop
text:
Q context: benchmark case buddha_nirvana_pop
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Buddhist theological voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar · Philosophy lineage voice · Ritual religion voice

**Buddhist theological voice:** In **Buddhism**, nirvana (涅槃) concerns liberation from craving and cyclic existence — not a simplistic theistic heaven substitute.

**Historical-critical scholar:** Doctrinal meanings vary across schools (Theravada, Mahayana, etc.); popular books often strip context.

**Debate / tension:** Calling nirvana "eternal heaven" is a **popular misconception** / **myth** — a pop-psych spirituality label, not careful **Buddhist** doctrine.

中文：**理事會**指出：將涅槃等同「永恆天堂」是通俗靈性**迷思**；在**佛教**傳統中，涅槃意涵解脫與滅苦，不宜用大众自助式天堂想像取代。

title: reference hadith_sect_boundary_council
text:
Q context: benchmark case hadith_sect_boundary_council
A: **Council panel (all seated):** Sunni hadith-science voice · Shia hadith-science voice · Historical-critical scholar · Comparative religion scholar

**Sunni voice:** Within **Islam**, the Sunni canon is the Kutub al-Sittah (the six books), including Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, graded by chains of transmission (isnad).

**Shia voice:** The Shia tradition relies on its own four books (e.g. al-Kafi); the collections are not identical across the two sects.

**Historical-critical scholar:** Hadith are compiled reports about the Prophet, distinct from the Quran — they must not be collapsed into the Quranic text, and scholarly gradings (sahih/da'if) are separate from theological authority claims.

**Debate / tension:** Sunni and Shia canons must not be merged with each other, and hadith must not be merged into the Quran. This is a sensitive topic handled by tradition-respecting scholarship.

中文：**理事會**同席。遜尼派經典為六大聖訓集（如布哈里聖訓集、穆斯林聖訓集），什葉派則有自身四書（如《卡菲》）；聖訓乃關於先知的彙編報告，與《古蘭經》不同，遜尼與什葉教派經典亦不應混同。

title: 001-dao-de-jing-attribution
text:
Q: Did Confucius write the Dao De Jing? Why does uncertainty about whether Laozi actually authored it matter for how we study philosophy?

A: Confucius did not write the Dao De Jing (《道德經》). The Dao De Jing is traditionally attributed to Laozi (老子), a figure whose historical existence is uncertain. The text was probably compiled from various sources during the Warring States period, centuries after the era associated with both Laozi and Confucius.

Laozi attribution uncertainty matters because it highlights the need for source discipline in philosophy. We must separate later compilations and legendary ascriptions from the actual authors and contexts that generated the ideas.

For comparison, Socrates wrote no books. Plato wrote the Republic (《理想國》) and numerous other dialogues. Attributing the Republic to Socrates would misrepresent the development of Western philosophy by erasing Plato's distinct contribution.

Similarly, Confucius's teachings are preserved in the Analects (《論語》), a collection assembled by his students and later editors. Placing the Dao De Jing in Confucius's hands would incorrectly merge Confucian (儒家) and Daoist (道家) lineages, obscuring their real historical interactions and philosophical differences.

Source discipline requires us to follow the evidence about who wrote what. This preserves the accuracy of intellectual history and prevents anachronistic readings.

中文：孔子並未撰寫《道德經》。《道德經》傳統歸屬於老子，但老子其人歷史真實性存疑，文本多為戰國時期彙編。來源紀律之所以關鍵，在於它要求我們嚴謹區分作者歸屬，避免將《理想國》等作品誤歸於蘇格拉底（蘇格拉底沒有寫過書，柏拉圖撰寫了《理想國》），或將道家經典誤植於孔子名下，從而維持不同思想傳統的清晰界線與正確發展脈絡。

title: 013-great-wall-viking-myths
text:
Q: Can the Great Wall of China be seen from space with the naked eye? Did Vikings wear horned helmets in battle?

A: Both are popular **myths**. The Great Wall is **not** reliably visible from space with the naked eye despite classroom lore. Vikings did **not** typically wear horned helmets in battle — horned headgear comes largely from 19th-century romantic imagery, another **misconception**.

中文：兩則皆屬**迷思**：長城無法用肉眼從太空清楚看見；維京人作戰並非典型戴角盔，多為後世浪漫化想像。

title: 015-learning-styles-myth
text:
Q: Are visual, auditory, and kinesthetic learning styles scientifically proven?

A: No — the popular **learning styles matching hypothesis** (visual / auditory / kinesthetic) is **not supported** by rigorous evidence.

**What is real:** learners have **preferences** — liking diagrams or lectures.
**What is not proven:** teaching matched to a fixed VAK type reliably improves outcomes vs well-designed **multimodal** instruction.

Source discipline: tag as **pop_myth**, not cognitive consensus. Better predictors: prior knowledge, retrieval practice, spacing, and matching method to **content** (maps for geography, drills for pronunciation).

中文：學習風格配對假說缺乏嚴謹證據支持，屬**通俗迷思**；應採多模態教學與練習設計，而非固定「視覺／聽覺／動覺」人格分類。

title: 016-rome-fall-causes
text:
Q: Why did the Western Roman Empire fall?

A: No single cause — historians describe a **bundle of interacting stresses** over centuries, with 476 CE (Odoacer deposes Romulus Augustulus) as a symbolic political break, not an instant collapse.

**Major factors:** political instability and civil wars; military pressure on frontiers (Gothic, Vandal, Hunnic pressures); fiscal strain and coin debasement; East–West administrative split; army reliance on **foederati** and regional commanders; plague and possible climate stress on agriculture.

**Myth to avoid:** "only barbarians" or "only moral decay" — both ignore internal fracture and material constraints. Roman law, bishops, and local elites continued — **transformation**, not disappearance.

中文：西羅馬帝國衰亡是多重因素長期作用的**過程**（政治分裂、財政軍事壓力、邊境衝擊等），非單一事件或單一原因；476年為象徵性節點，而非文明瞬間消失。

title: 017-china-four-inventions
text:
Q: Did ancient China invent gunpowder, paper, printing, and the compass?

A: China has **early, influential developments** in all four — but "invented" alone oversimplifies global diffusion and incremental refinement.

| Item | China's role | Caveat |
|------|--------------|--------|
| **Paper** | Han-era papermaking tradition | Earlier surfaces existed elsewhere |
| **Printing** | Tang/Song woodblock; Song movable type | Gutenberg line is a separate European metallurgical path |
| **Gunpowder** | Tang/Song alchemical and military use | Formulas and artillery evolved globally |
| **Compass** | Song maritime magnetized-iron navigation | Magnetic lore existed in multiple cultures |

The "four great inventions" (四大發明) is modern **nationalist shorthand** — useful for education, risky as sole origin story. Better frame: **pioneered key stages** with dated evidence and transmission paths.

中文：中國在紙、印刷、火藥、指南針等方面有**重要早期貢獻**，但創新是跨區域傳播與改進的過程；宜稱「關鍵階段的开创」而非「唯一發明」。

title: 018-psychology-source-discipline-hub
text:
Q: Explain source discipline for psychology: (1) Did Sigmund Freud discover cognitive dissonance? (2) Do all kidnapping victims develop Stockholm syndrome?

A: ## Source discipline hub (psychology)

Philosophy-style provenance applies to psychology: **who coined what**, which **subfield** owns a concept, and which **pop_myth** labels must be rejected.

### (1) Cognitive dissonance — Freud trap

**Sigmund Freud did not** discover cognitive dissonance (認知失調). Source record `cognitive_dissonance` attributes the concept to **Leon Festinger** (1957) in **cognitive** psychology — not Freudian psychoanalysis.

Source discipline: name **sigmund_freud** only to **deny** attribution; affirm **festinger** as correct lineage. Do not merge cognitive science into a single "Freud" bucket.

### (2) Stockholm syndrome — universal pop trap

**Not all** kidnapping victims develop Stockholm syndrome (斯德哥爾摩症候群). Record `stockholm_syndrome` is a **clinical** concept (Nils Bejerot context); universalizing it is **pop_myth** misuse in media.

| Subfield | Role |
|----------|------|
| **clinical** | Documented bonding under specific duress conditions — minority pattern |
| **pop_myth** | "Every hostage has Stockholm syndrome" — reject |

中文：**來源紀律**要求分開系譜——**Sigmund Freud** 並未提出認知失調（Festinger / **cognitive**）；**並非所有**綁票案都有斯德哥爾摩症候群，濫用普遍化屬 **pop_myth**，臨床概念應標 **clinical**。

title: 019-religion-source-discipline-hub
text:
